1db61e77-786d-4d4c-94e0-4dcc221af20b.product_description: Challenges of execution post-launch in AI-driven development

prediction.review_text:
After launching an AI-driven solution, I've found that the real challenge often begins with execution. 🚀 It's not just about deploying the technology; it's about ensuring we have robust systems in place that can adapt and thrive in real-world scenarios. I've observed many projects falter post-launch due to overlooked operational complexities and a lack of alignment within teams.

Here’s my approach to overcoming these challenges:
1. **Plan for Change**: Always anticipate the need for adjustments and develop a clear strategy to address them.
2. **Monitor Outcomes**: Establish metrics that provide insights into performance, enabling data-driven decisions.
3. **Foster Team Engagement**: Encourage open communication within your team to swiftly address issues as they arise.

Let’s focus on building systems that not only function but excel in delivering consistent results! 💪 #AI #Automation #TechStrategy #Execution

actual.review_text:
AI speeds up the first version.
It slows down the first real change.

Teams use AI to scaffold code and ship quickly.
The initial release looks fine.

Then something concrete changes:

→ pricing logic needs an update
→ a new user role needs access
→ one workflow behaves differently for a customer

That’s when execution stalls.
Not because development is slow,

but because no one is fully sure:
 
→ why certain decisions were made
→ what parts are safe to modify
→ what will break if they touch it

AI reduced the cost of getting to version one.
It didn’t reduce the cost of understanding the system.

Execution problems don’t appear at launch.
They appear at the first unavoidable change.

topics_ordered: impact of AI on education accessibility
need for tool integration in businesses

actual.review_text:
Interacting with different business owners every day made me realize a major shift.

Most businesses don’t need another tool, they just need their existing tools to talk to each other.

That’s what modern orchestration really is, not coding, not AI magic, but connecting everything so it works in sync.

Imagine invoices arriving by email, getting verified automatically, updating your accounts, and sending a Slack update without anyone touching a spreadsheet.

That’s exactly what we’ve been helping businesses achieve using our ready-to-use orchestration templates.

It doesn’t have to be complex or expensive anymore, it just has to be connected.

I recently wrote about a real example of this in action, You can read the full story below.

If you’re exploring how orchestration could simplify your business operations, feel free to reach out. I’d love to share what’s working for others.

You can also book a quick discovery call here: [

Or take a look at what some of our clients have achieved: [

#BusinessGrowth #WorkflowOrchestration #DigitalTransformation #NoCode #n8n #Operations #OrchestrationTemplates

actual.review_text:
Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

#sastic

🚀 Excited to share some insights on learning paths in programming and software development! As someone who's navigated various roles from QA to engineering at Microsoft, I can attest to the importance of structured learning. Whether you're diving into .NET, exploring Azure, or mastering React, having a clear roadmap can make all the difference. 

I encourage everyone to leverage resources like Pluralsight and GitHub for hands-on tutorials and projects. Remember, the journey is just as important as the destination! Let's keep supporting each other in our growth. 💪 #CareerDevelopment #SoftwareEngineering #Mentorship

actual.review_text:
I've successfully completed the learning path "Become a C++ Developer" from LinkedIn Learning, a learning path which consists of over 17 hours of C++ theory and in-depth exercises!

My favorite module of the learning path was and still is "Web Servers and APIs Using C++", by Troy Miles.

The module was outstanding, I've learnt how to create and automate Web Servers with C++, deploy resources using Docker, include HTML5, CSS3 and PHP elements and, last but not least, integrate external resources such as MongoDB to the Web Servers.

I'm very grateful for everything that I've learnt and this is just the start!

I recommend the learning path "Become a C++ Developer" for every C++ passionate programmer and for everyone else who would like to enrichen their C++ knowledge.

Link to the LinkedIn Learning Path: 

Thank you, everyone!

#developer #docker #programming #cplusplus #objectorientedprogramming #linkedinlearning

actual.review_text:
Earlier this week, I had the opportunity of interviewing with CNBC Squawk Box Asia to discuss some of our latest Work Trend Index findings. I’m excited for what’s around the corner with hybrid work and staying close with customers to learn more from how they continue to navigate some of the biggest challenges from the past year. Some major takeaways to think about :

Gen Z, women and underrepresented minorities have been most negatively affected in terms of career advancement, feeling engaged/excited about work, and getting a word in during meetings.

Hybrid work is going to be the new normal. 70% want flexible work to stay, while over 60% want more in-person time and more than 65% of leaders plan to redesign office space for hybrid work.

Hear more from the interview here: 
#HybridWork #FutureofWork #RemoteWork

actual.review_text:
Centralized Customer Payments for Headquarters and Subsidiaries by Komathi T SAP Labs India 

Welcome to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Two-tier ERP blog series. 

In this blog, let’s look at centralized customer payments in a Two-tier deployment model.

S/4HANA customer payments is a public cloud solution delivered in the SAP Cloud Platform, which provides a self-service offering to enable our customers to deploy electronic bill presentment and payment processes. In this Two-tier scenario, where headquarters is running On-Premise (ECC6+ or S/4HANA On-premise) and the subsidiary is running on S/4HANA cloud, SAP S/4HANA cloud for customer payment serves as a centralized customer payment solution for both headquarter as well as subsidiary.

While running in such two-tier landscape, processing repetitive customer inquiries regarding invoice copies, underpayment, dispute cases, matching incoming payments with open items because of Time zone issues, Language barriers, Large numbers of documents would be a great challenge to the accounts team.

Check out the full blog here

prediction.review_text:
I've been diving deep into web development with Django and React lately, and it's been an enlightening experience! 🌟 Here are some insights I've gathered along the way:

1. **Embrace Scalability**: Selecting the right framework is pivotal. It’s not just about the current project but how it will adapt as your application grows. Always think ahead!
2. **Community is Key**: The support I've received from fellow developers has been invaluable. Collaborating, troubleshooting, and sharing resources can significantly elevate our projects and foster innovation.
3. **Integration Matters**: Ensuring your framework works seamlessly with your existing tech stack can enhance productivity and streamline workflows. It's all about creating a cohesive environment for development.

I’m eager to hear your experiences! What frameworks have you found transformative in your projects? Let’s connect and share our insights in this vibrant tech community! #WebDevelopment #Django #React #TechCommunity #LearningTogether

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
Building web applications has never been more accessible, thanks to modern development frameworks! 🚀 Whether you're leveraging React for dynamic UIs or Django for robust backends, the right tools can significantly accelerate your development process. 

Here are three key considerations when choosing a framework:
1. **Scalability**: Ensure it can handle growth as your user base expands.
2. **Community Support**: A strong community means more resources and troubleshooting help.
3. **Integration Capabilities**: Look for frameworks that easily integrate with your existing tech stack.

What frameworks have you found most effective? Let's share insights and help each other grow! #WebDevelopment #TechCommunity #LearningTogether

actual.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.

1db61e77-786d-4d4c-94e0-4dcc221af20b_rev.product_description: Challenges of execution post-launch in AI-driven development

actual_review_data.review_text:
AI speeds up the first version.
It slows down the first real change.

Teams use AI to scaffold code and ship quickly.
The initial release looks fine.

Then something concrete changes:

→ pricing logic needs an update
→ a new user role needs access
→ one workflow behaves differently for a customer

That’s when execution stalls.
Not because development is slow,

but because no one is fully sure:
 
→ why certain decisions were made
→ what parts are safe to modify
→ what will break if they touch it

AI reduced the cost of getting to version one.
It didn’t reduce the cost of understanding the system.

Execution problems don’t appear at launch.
They appear at the first unavoidable change.
actual_review_data.product_description: Challenges of execution post-launch in AI-driven development

initial_prediction_data.review_text:
After launching an AI-driven solution, the real challenge often begins: execution. 🚀 It's not just about deploying the tech; it's about ensuring reliability and repeatability in real-world scenarios. I’ve seen too many projects stumble post-launch due to overlooked operational complexities. 

Here’s what I recommend:
1. **Plan for Change**: Anticipate the need for adjustments and have a strategy in place.
2. **Monitor Outcomes**: Use metrics to gauge performance and make data-driven decisions.
3. **Engage Your Team**: Foster open communication to address issues as they arise.

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

#sastic

7333df73-63cf-4f92-b724-caefc9171b63_rev.product_description: Learning paths in programming and software development

actual_review_data.review_text:
I've successfully completed the learning path "Become a C++ Developer" from LinkedIn Learning, a learning path which consists of over 17 hours of C++ theory and in-depth exercises!

My favorite module of the learning path was and still is "Web Servers and APIs Using C++", by Troy Miles.

The module was outstanding, I've learnt how to create and automate Web Servers with C++, deploy resources using Docker, include HTML5, CSS3 and PHP elements and, last but not least, integrate external resources such as MongoDB to the Web Servers.

I'm very grateful for everything that I've learnt and this is just the start!

I recommend the learning path "Become a C++ Developer" for every C++ passionate programmer and for everyone else who would like to enrichen their C++ knowledge.

Link to the LinkedIn Learning Path: 

Thank you, everyone!

#developer #docker #programming #cplusplus #objectorientedprogramming #linkedinlearning

actual_review_data.review_text:
Earlier this week, I had the opportunity of interviewing with CNBC Squawk Box Asia to discuss some of our latest Work Trend Index findings. I’m excited for what’s around the corner with hybrid work and staying close with customers to learn more from how they continue to navigate some of the biggest challenges from the past year. Some major takeaways to think about :

Gen Z, women and underrepresented minorities have been most negatively affected in terms of career advancement, feeling engaged/excited about work, and getting a word in during meetings.

Hybrid work is going to be the new normal. 70% want flexible work to stay, while over 60% want more in-person time and more than 65% of leaders plan to redesign office space for hybrid work.

Hear more from the interview here: 
#HybridWork #FutureofWork #RemoteWork

dc0b5d74-87d9-4fe3-8883-aef9d0924114_rev.product_description: Centralized customer payments in ERP systems

actual_review_data.review_text:
Centralized Customer Payments for Headquarters and Subsidiaries by Komathi T SAP Labs India 

Welcome to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Two-tier ERP blog series. 

In this blog, let’s look at centralized customer payments in a Two-tier deployment model.

S/4HANA customer payments is a public cloud solution delivered in the SAP Cloud Platform, which provides a self-service offering to enable our customers to deploy electronic bill presentment and payment processes. In this Two-tier scenario, where headquarters is running On-Premise (ECC6+ or S/4HANA On-premise) and the subsidiary is running on S/4HANA cloud, SAP S/4HANA cloud for customer payment serves as a centralized customer payment solution for both headquarter as well as subsidiary.

While running in such two-tier landscape, processing repetitive customer inquiries regarding invoice copies, underpayment, dispute cases, matching incoming payments with open items because of Time zone issues, Language barriers, Large numbers of documents would be a great challenge to the accounts team.

Check out the full blog here

actual_review_data.review_text:
Not many of you may know but I spent a lot of my career in the pathology laboratories doing software engineering.

I have often thought how AI will revolutionize patient care, especially in terms of diagnostic services such as radiology and pathology. So it was exciting to see new research from my current employer Microsoft and my former, the UW, rolling research into exactly this, using AI to improve cancer outcomes.

Thinking of the good old days when Rodney Schmidt and I were trying "change the world" with software!

f20ab826-b48b-42cf-be34-de640b4befa1_rev.product_description: Routing management capabilities in cloud environments

actual_review_data.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.
